Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Oh! Happy Valentine\xe2\x80\x99s Day. \xe2\x9d\xa4\xef\xb8\x8f\xf0\x9f\xa9\xb7\xf0\x9f\x92\x9c Sarah \xe2\x80\x9cSShapiro@puck.news\xe2\x80\x9d Shapiro spent the week in New York seeing shows, visiting showrooms, and walking the grounds of just about every store on the island of Manhattan in order to share this totally subjective, absolutely unscientific, and incredibly insightful channel check on what\xe2\x80\x99s happening in retail at this very moment

Feb 14, 2025

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Oh! Happy Valentine\xe2\x80\x99s Day. \xe2\x9d\xa4\xef\xb8\x8f\xf0\x9f\xa9\xb7\xf0\x9f\x92\x9c

Sarah \xe2\x80\x9cSShapiro@puck.news\xe2\x80\x9d Shapiro spent the week in New York seeing shows , visiting showrooms , and walking the grounds of just about every store on the island of Manhattan in order to share this totally subjective, absolutely unscientific, and incredibly insightful channel check on what\xe2\x80\x99s happening in retail at this very moment\xe2\x80\x94from the rapid rise of Toteme to the inevitable demise of Long-Island-young-mom shoe brand Golden Goose (thank goodness). Up top, a little on what\xe2\x80\x99s happening in Europe, from Herm\xc3\xa8s\xe2\x80\x99s and Moncler\xe2\x80\x99s extremely sweet earnings results to the latest at Michael Rider \xe2\x80\x99s Celine.

Also: Thanks to everyone who upgraded to an Inner Circle membership yesterday in order to read our H.R. scoops and my proprietary intel on the business of Oscar de la Renta . If you\xe2\x80\x99re somehow still not a regular ol\xe2\x80\x99 Puck subscriber , I kind of don\xe2\x80\x99t know what to say to you at this point? The benefits are vast, from improved mental health to a higher I.Q. (As for E.Q.: I can\xe2\x80\x99t help you with that.)

Mentioned in this issue : Michael Rider , Celine, Kering, Francesca Bellettini , Gucci, Louis Vuitton, Herm\xc3\xa8s, Remo Ruffini , Moncler, LVMH, Axel Dumas , Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s, Ralph Lauren, Adidas, Kendrick Lamar , flares (again), Sandy Liang , Banana Republic, Pete Davidson , boyfriend couches, Aritzia, J.W. Anderson, Zara, J.Crew, and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

The all-electric BMW i7 merges technology and design with an available Panoramic Sky Lounge LED Roof and 4D surround sound. Learn more at BMWUSA.com .

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • The week in luxury : The big news out of Paris this morning is that Michael Rider has set his Celine debut for July 6, right before the start of the Couture shows. I confirmed with people familiar with Rider\xe2\x80\x99s plans that the show is slated to be menswear and womenswear: a complete explainer of his vision for Celine. I can\xe2\x80\x99t wait, and I\xe2\x80\x99m sure you can\xe2\x80\x99t either. (The men\xe2\x80\x99s shows in June and the Couture shows in July are going to be good .)

    Meanwhile, a bunch of European companies released their latest earnings over the past few days. At Kering, despite still-lagging sales, Francesca Bellettini remains convinced that Gucci can be as big as Louis Vuitton or Herm\xc3\xa8s, with time. And you won\xe2\x80\x99t be surprised that Herm\xc3\xa8s was up double digits\xe2\x80\x94more than 22 percent in the U.S. alone in its fourth quarter. C.E.O. Axel Dumas also addressed the challenges the business is facing, from potential tariffs to \xe2\x80\x9cWirkins\xe2\x80\x9d and other dupes, which he said the company will continue to combat even though there\xe2\x80\x99s an argument that knockoffs and counterfeits have no real effect on revenues.

    As for Moncler, they beat expectations in China, where pretty much everyone is still struggling. C.E.O. Remo Ruffini also wanted to make clear that, despite LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s 10 percent stake in his personal investment vehicle\xe2\x80\x94which in turn controls more than 15 percent of the group, which also includes Stone Island (hi, Robert Triefus )\xe2\x80\x94Moncler remains \xe2\x80\x9cfully independent.\xe2\x80\x9d Finally, WWD has news of a Richemont executive shakeup, including the exit of beauty head Boet Brinkgreve , which I already reported back in November 2024. \xe2\x80\x94 Lauren Sherman

Now, here\xe2\x80\x99s Sarah\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s x Dimes Square : The positioning of a brand on a department-store floor is a tricky thing: You want to put like-minded, similarly priced brands together so that you catch the right consumer, but often brands are supersensitive about their physical placement: not only the exact spot they occupy, but also who\xe2\x80\x99s next to them. That\xe2\x80\x99s why I thought it was interesting that Ralph Lauren is now on the second floor of Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s 59th Street flagship, after a longtime stint on the third floor alongside\xe2\x80\x94no offense\xe2\x80\x94fuddy-duddy brands like Eileen Fisher and the private label C by Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s, a cashmere collection. (In merchandiser parlance, these brands are called \xe2\x80\x9cmoderate.\xe2\x80\x9d A long time ago, they used to be called \xe2\x80\x9cbridge,\xe2\x80\x9d but I haven\xe2\x80\x99t heard someone use that term since the 1990s.)

    The second floor is the home of Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s contemporary offering. Contemporary is also an outdated term that doesn\xe2\x80\x99t mean much these days, but what it essentially imparts is that a brand is younger and cooler than \xe2\x80\x9cmoderate\xe2\x80\x9d lines, but more gently priced than designer goods. Given that Ralph Lauren has become a cool brand with Gen Z\xe2\x80\x94thanks in part to the latter\xe2\x80\x99s ability to buy it for cheap and secondhand on marketplaces like Etsy\xe2\x80\x94it\xe2\x80\x99s understandable that Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s wants to put it front and center on Floor 2, where the kids living in Bushwick go when they need an interview outfit. This is a significant development, since planograms are serious business: Space plans involve weighing a mix of revenue per square foot, brand interest, company politics, how good the merchandise looks besides other brands, etcetera. \xe2\x80\x94 Sarah Shapiro
  • Golden Goose lays egg : Remember when intentionally scuffed-up Golden Goose sneakers were the ultimate spendy, upper-crust, desirable sneaker in the West Hollywood\xe2\x80\x93West Village\xe2\x80\x93Manhasset axis of evil? Well, styles have switched up yet again. Just don\xe2\x80\x99t tell Hong Kong\xe2\x80\x99s Blue Pool, an Alibaba portfolio company, which just snagged a 12 percent stake in the Italian brand. (Alas, an I.P.O. hasn\xe2\x80\x99t been in the cards.) Anyway, as I walked through Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s, Bergdorf Goodman, Nordstrom, and Saks this week, I was shocked at the prime real estate still dedicated to these dirty birds (my friend\xe2\x80\x99s coinage). In the late 2010s and early 2020s, stores built shrines to this S.K.U.-heavy brand. But now the ship has sailed, and my bet is the inventory is pretty aged at this point. It\xe2\x80\x99s hard to get rid of that much stock all at once.

    The latest trend in luxury sneakers has been driven by the impressive resurgence of the Adidas Samba , which has ascended to Line Sheet cinematic universe status of late. This retro European football sneaker became so popular that it sparked a broader obsession with low-profile, vintage-inspired kicks\xe2\x80\x94most notably, the German Army Trainer (GAT). In fact, the GAT has become the insider\xe2\x80\x99s alternative to the ubiquitous Samba\xe2\x80\x94the embodiment of a retro wave of quiet luxury and minimalism espoused by luxury designer brands that weren\xe2\x80\x99t really playing the sneaker game. The GAT silhouette has been influencing trends on and off since the \xe2\x80\x9970s, when it first came onto the scene. This sneaker is versatile, a great travel shoe, and sans a splashy logo, all of which makes it a great business-casual, work-appropriate option.

    Indeed, this wave of retro minimalism has caught the attention of luxury\xe2\x80\x99s most discerning players. The Row \xe2\x80\x99s sneaker looked absolutely at home on Bergdorf Goodman\xe2\x80\x99s shoe floor. Dries Van Noten\xe2\x80\x99s simple suede version now comes in mesh for spring, and stores are constantly selling out in key sizes. And Proenza Schouler crafted a sneaker that is super clean and simple\xe2\x80\x94not a speck of glitter in sight. \xe2\x80\x94 Sarah Shapiro

And now, the main event\xe2\x80\xa6

The Week in Shopping: NYFW Channel Check Edition

News and notes on Manhattan retail trends amid the concurrent post-holiday malaise and NYFW exodus.

Sarah Shapiro

I spent the past week doing the New York Marathon equivalent of channel checks. It\xe2\x80\x99s still freezing here, so I saw plenty of outerwear (a lot of which will be returned to vendors at the end of the season) in addition to the spring deliveries that are already manifesting: more light blues and plenty of stripes to signal warmer days to come.

At Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s this week, I had a flashback to the days when I was an assistant buyer required to wear all-black suits when walking the floor\xe2\x80\x94a uniform inevitably made up of Theory, the definitive brand for corporate women in those days. The brand\xe2\x80\x99s style hasn\xe2\x80\x99t changed much since the late \xe2\x80\x9990s and early 2000s, when its three-season wool suits became a wardrobe necessity for the era\xe2\x80\x99s power-dressing women wishing to project authority while maintaining femininity in predominantly male corporate environments. (That is, if you couldn\xe2\x80\x99t afford Helmut Lang or Jil Sander.)

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

The all-electric BMW i7 merges technology and design with an available Panoramic Sky Lounge LED Roof and 4D surround sound. Learn more at BMWUSA.com .

Theory\xe2\x80\x99s dominance has waned as workplace dress codes have relaxed and the market has evolved. But people still need to dress nicely when they go to work, even if a suit isn\xe2\x80\x99t the answer. What is, then? My theory: Toteme . Everyone is wearing it: People who wear The Row, but feel it\xe2\x80\x99s getting too expensive; people who wear Uniqlo, but need better fabrics for Monday through Thursday; fashion obsessives who simply view designer Elin Kling \xe2\x80\x99s vision as the best basics around. Where Theory once symbolized assimilating corporate culture, Toteme\xe2\x80\x99s thoughtful Scandinavian minimalism is a better brand for this era of peak individualism: Sure, everybody might still be wearing the same thing, but they want to believe they\xe2\x80\x99re different, and Toteme\xe2\x80\x99s curved-seam jeans and oh-so-particular boot shape help to maintain that illusion.

Toteme exemplifies another trend that\xe2\x80\x99s pervasive on the floors this season. Like TWP and Kallmeyer , Toteme is succeeding in the increasingly crowded and competitive \xe2\x80\x9caccessible luxury\xe2\x80\x9d price band. Pricing, of course, is an art as well as a science\xe2\x80\x94but The Row\xe2\x80\x99s and Loro Piana\xe2\x80\x99s decision to elasticize price upwards, inspired by Herm\xc3\xa8s, doesn\xe2\x80\x99t mean everyone else has carte blanche to follow this trend. Yes, there are material, labor, marketing costs, etcetera to consider, but prices also send a signal about who the product is for . The Totemes of the world have nabbed the working woman\xe2\x80\x99s functional wardrobe, and their holistic marriage of pricing, style, and quality offer a good lesson to others.

Here\xe2\x80\x99s What Else I\xe2\x80\x99m Seeing

Miu Miu\xe2\x80\x99s effect on the high street is unparalleled. The brand\xe2\x80\x99s layered, polychrome swim trunks with prominent string ties worn under trousers have created a domino effect at the mall, with Zara , Aritzia , Reformation , Monse , and Simkhai all offering a version.

Also, I\xe2\x80\x99m confident that we\xe2\x80\x99ve reached peak cropped dress shirt, inspired by TWP \xe2\x80\x99s now years-old version. The latest rounds I saw were at Alex Mill , J.W. Anderson for Uniqlo , J.Crew , Gap , Athleta , Madewell , and yes, new versions at TWP . Quince\xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9ccropped\xe2\x80\x9d shirt isn\xe2\x80\x99t really cropped, but that\xe2\x80\x99s what watered-down merchandising often becomes when you try to do \xe2\x80\x9ctrend\xe2\x80\x9d for a mass audience.

Perhaps surprisingly, the store offering the most excitement during my NYC spree was, by far, Aritzia. They\xe2\x80\x99re doing many things right, starting with clothes that look good\xe2\x80\x94nothing groundbreaking, but no need for that anyway. Aritzia does this by building distinct sub-brands that fit for different use cases or shoppers, Urban Outfitters style. They\xe2\x80\x99ve captured a Gen Z audience with a \xe2\x80\x9c corporate fetish \xe2\x80\x9d look that\xe2\x80\x99s fitted and updated without being too sexy for the office. One delivery on the other side of the trend spectrum had old-school, Abercrombie & Fitch, LoveShackFancy notes, with ruffled skirts and cinched ruffled poplin tanks . There are also sweat sets , wool coats , and their bestselling Super Puff coats . Their \xe2\x80\x9990s style Prada-ish nylon bags are priced appropriately for a logo-free simple bag and feel like a no-brainer, easy add-on at checkout.

When I was there, people were actually shopping and engaging with the product. Aritzia made the experience fun. In the store, they\xe2\x80\x99ve got a giant Super Puff coat\xe2\x80\x94cartoonishly oversize even with two adults inside\xe2\x80\x94the point being to take selfies, post to social media, and tag the store. The \xe2\x80\x9cboyfriend\xe2\x80\x9d couch (which another retailer, Reformation, just dropped into a fun Pete Davidson campaign) was occupied by drowsy wingmen.

Lastly, it\xe2\x80\x99s worth noting that the runway-to-resale pipeline is reshaping how consumers react to Fashion Week: Why wait for the designer interpretation when you can snag the original vintage piece that inspired it? When Sandy Liang sends a retro fleece quarter-zip down the runway, or Kendrick Lamar wears flare jeans at the Super Bowl halftime show, savvy shoppers aren\xe2\x80\x99t waiting six months to spend their money on something new\xe2\x80\x94they\xe2\x80\x99re heading straight to The RealReal, eBay, and other resale spots to score authentic originals. The irony isn\xe2\x80\x99t lost on industry insiders. As brands continue to mine the archives for inspiration\xe2\x80\x94 without significantly updating the references\xe2\x80\x94they\xe2\x80\x99re inadvertently driving customers to resale platforms, which see their inventory surge every time a designer plays musical chairs, new trends emerge, and a throwback is front and center again.

The Week in Feedback\xe2\x80\xa6

On Kendrick\xe2\x80\x99s Super Bowl jeans, be they Celine or American Eagle : \xe2\x80\x9cI feel like there\xe2\x80\x99s some Gallery Dept. erasure re: the flared jeans.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 Kaitlin Phillips , who thinks it\xe2\x80\x99s lame that people who share feedback want to be anonymous

On buying the aforementioned jean s: \xe2\x80\x9cThe first thing I did this morning was order those Celine jeans. There were conflicting reports on whether it was the Serge or the Surf style, but I went with the Serge because they were $550 on 24s.com and in a full range of sizes, unlike the Surf, which were $1,200 to $1,300 depending on the website. [ Ed. note : The Serge has since sold out on 24s.com.] While I was on this bootcut high, I also ordered the Nili Lotan Roe jean, as it looks to be a good flare but with a little darker wash. Will report back on all fronts.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A cool lawyer lady

On Bode\xe2\x80\x99s off-piste show : \xe2\x80\x9cBode in NOLA being a Fashion Week moment means something. With so little energy in New York, I\xe2\x80\x99m wondering if brands are going to follow [ Emily \xe2\x80\x99s] lead and start staging shows in interesting secondary markets where they can get (tastefully) referential with their designs and court a new customer base while still keeping the attention of the fashion world. Yes, the Super Bowl is a B.F.D. and gave lots of folks reason to follow, but I wonder, in the age of digital, if it wouldn\xe2\x80\x99t work sans hoopla.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 Libby Callaway, the patron saint of Nashville fashion

On New York mag\xe2\x80\x99s Carl Swanson replacement : \xe2\x80\x9c Erik Maza is such a good fit and great for me, personally, who just subscribed and loves a good power issue. The more ex- Observer people working on that, the better.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A brand consultant

On the rise of TWP : \xe2\x80\x9cI have been a shopper for a while.\xc2\xa0 When you finally nail your size (everything is big ), it\xe2\x80\x99s so super chic. My Gen X friends and younger (thank you) are all buying pieces. But just like Khaite, let\xe2\x80\x99s see how cool fashion \xe2\x80\x98affordable\xe2\x80\x99 plays against almost luxury pricing with similar silhouettes. TWP has to hold prices to take the sales from Phoebe and The Row and Khaite for some of the chicest basics.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94An executive whom I once referred to as a Boomer but who is actually Gen X

Have a great weekend,
Lauren

P.S. : We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off them.
